title: crotalaria thaumasiophylla
intro: Crotalaria thaumasiophylla, also known as the golden bush or golden rattlebox, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities. Its health benefits include its potential use in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its role in wound healing and anti-inflammatory processes. Crotalaria thaumasiophylla is traditionally prepared as an infusion or decoction for herbal remedies, although its exact uses and preparations may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
